Key_Milestone: Imatinib (Gleevec) FDA approved for CML — first targeted TKI
Bortezomib (Velcade) shows efficacy in relapsed MM trials
Bortezomib FDA approved for relapsed MM — first proteasome inhibitor
WHO classification updated; MDS/MPN entities refined
Lenalidomide FDA approved for MDS with del(5q)
Lenalidomide + Dex approved for relapsed MM
Dasatinib and Nilotinib approved as 2nd-gen TKIs for CML
JAK2 V617F testing becomes standard for MPN diagnosis
Bendamustine + Rituximab becomes standard for CLL
Pomalidomide enters clinical trials for MM
Ruxolitinib FDA approved for myelofibrosis — first JAK inhibitor
Ibrutinib breakthrough therapy designation for CLL
Ibrutinib and Idelalisib FDA approved for CLL/MCL
Blinatumomab FDA approved for ALL — first bispecific antibody
Daratumumab and Elotuzumab FDA approved for MM; CAR-T trials expand
Venetoclax FDA approved for CLL with del(17p)
Tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah) FDA approved — first CAR-T for ALL
Ivosidenib (IDH1) and Enasidenib (IDH2) approved for AML; Gilteritinib (FLT3)
Venetoclax + Azacitidine approved for unfit AML; Fixed-duration CLL regimens emerge
COVID-19 pandemic disrupts cancer care globally; Isatuximab approved for MM
Idecabtagene vicleucel (Ide-cel) — first CAR-T for MM; Luspatercept for MDS anemia
Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Cilta-cel) approved for MM; Teclistamab (first bispecific for MM); WHO 2022 classification update
Talquetamab approved for MM; Imetelstat for MDS; BET inhibitors in trials
Multiple CAR-T products expand to earlier MM lines; AI-driven genomics for MDS risk stratification
Fixed-duration triplet regimens becoming standard in CLL; CRISPR-edited cell therapies enter Phase II trials for leukemia
Next-gen bispecifics and off-the-shelf CAR-NK trials underway; MRD-guided treatment adaptation
